GET /radar/traffic_anomalies.description: Retrieves the latest Internet traffic anomalies, which are signals that might indicate an outage. These alerts are automatically detected by Radar and manually verified by our team.

parameters.description: Limits the number of objects returned in the response.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Limits the number of objects returned in the response.

parameters.description: Skips the specified number of objects before fetching the results.
parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Skips the specified number of objects before fetching the results.

parameters.description: Filters results by a relative date range ending at the current time. Use `<n>d` for days (up to `364d`) or `<n>w` for weeks (up to `52w`), e.g. `7d`. Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `3dcontrol` covers days -10 to -7, `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `28dcontrol` covers days -56 to -28, and `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). Mutually exclusive with `dateStart`/`dateEnd`.
parameters.name: dateRange

schema.description: Filters results by a relative date range ending at the current time. Use `<n>d` for days (up to `364d`) or `<n>w` for weeks (up to `52w`), e.g. `7d`. Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `3dcontrol` covers days -10 to -7, `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `28dcontrol` covers days -56 to -28, and `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). Mutually exclusive with `dateStart`/`dateEnd`.

parameters.description: Start of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`.
parameters.name: dateStart

schema.description: Start of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`.

parameters.description: End of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateStart`.
parameters.name: dateEnd

schema.description: End of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateStart`.

parameters.description: Filters results by entity type (LOCATION, AS, or ORIGIN).
parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filters results by entity type (LOCATION, AS, or ORIGIN).

parameters.description: Filters results by Autonomous System. Specify a single Autonomous System Number (ASN) as integer.
parameters.name: asn

schema.description: Filters results by Autonomous System. Specify a single Autonomous System Number (ASN) as integer.

parameters.description: Filters results by location. Specify an alpha-2 location code.
parameters.name: location

schema.description: Filters results by location. Specify an alpha-2 location code.

parameters.description: Format in which results will be returned.
parameters.name: format

schema.description: Format in which results will be returned.

GET /radar/traffic_anomalies/locations.description: Retrieves the sum of Internet traffic anomalies, grouped by location. These anomalies are signals that might indicate an outage, automatically detected by Radar and manually verified by our team.

parameters.description: Limits the number of objects returned in the response.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Limits the number of objects returned in the response.

parameters.description: Filters results by a relative date range ending at the current time. Use `<n>d` for days (up to `364d`) or `<n>w` for weeks (up to `52w`), e.g. `7d`. Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `3dcontrol` covers days -10 to -7, `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `28dcontrol` covers days -56 to -28, and `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). Mutually exclusive with `dateStart`/`dateEnd`.
parameters.name: dateRange

Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `3dcontrol` covers days -10 to -7, `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `28dcontrol` covers days -56 to -28, and `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). Mutually exclusive with `dateStart`/`dateEnd`.

parameters.description: Start of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`.
parameters.name: dateStart

schema.description: Start of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`.

parameters.name: dateEnd

parameters.description: Format in which results will be returned.
parameters.name: format

schema.description: Format in which results will be returned.

200.description: List of locations with number of traffic anomalies.

GET /radar/traffic_anomalies/locations.summary: Get top locations by total traffic anomalies
